# Questions in Livestream 185

Live Stream #185: Song of the Awesome

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=1030" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:10</a> | Did ancient Egyptians ever explore old ruins and temples the way later archaeologists do? | Sometimes. He said major elites such as Khaemwaset are famous for restoring and investigating older monuments, though ordinary people usually did not do this in any systematic way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=1459" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:19</a> | Is God eternally existent, or did he create himself out of primordial chaos? | He rejected self-creation and described God as eternal and outside time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=1921" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:01</a> | How did Abraham relate to God before the temple and formal laws? | He says Abraham could sacrifice without a temple because pre-Sinai worship worked differently, including standing stones and earlier forms of worship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=2283" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">38:03</a> | Does the Bible prohibit Christian YouTubers from interviewing false teachers? | He works through 2 John and Revelation 2 and treats the issue as about endorsement and platforming, not every kind of conversation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=2735" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:35</a> | Are there Sunday school misconceptions about Daniel in the lions' den? | He says the story itself is less the issue than broader assumptions about Daniel, including eunuch-related traditions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=3126" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">52:06</a> | Can unbelievers minimize their punishment in hell by good deeds? | He says biblical recompense language is often about punishment according to works, and he works through the cited passages cautiously. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=3466" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:46</a> | Why were the Amarna kings scratched from history? | He points to Atenism as corrupt worship and to political-religious discomfort with that period. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=3905" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:05</a> | What would Yahweh look like in Phoenician script? | He says he is not an expert on Phoenician script, then sketches a cautious comparison with related Proto-Sinaitic/Phoenician forms. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=4234" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:10:34</a> | How do you make a D&D 2e character sheet? | He says to start with a template, roll the stats, and fill in class, spells, saves, and related fields. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=4677" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:17:57</a> | What does he think of publishing saints' lives in comic form? | He says hagiography can have a legitimate teaching role if done without unhelpful baggage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=4888" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:28</a> | Does he know any good Christian manga? | He says he knows some exist but cannot remember titles off the top of his head. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=4957" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:37</a> | Is it unholy to take medication during pregnancy or birth? | He treats this as a question about pain, the curse, and medical relief, and does not frame medication as inherently unholy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=5344" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:04</a> | How is Ephesians 1:21 talking about different kinds of angelic or spiritual beings? | He connects Ephesians 1 with Ephesians 6, Romans 8, and 1 Peter 3 to show the terms refer to spiritual powers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=5682" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:42</a> | What is the meaning behind Jude quoting 1 Enoch? | He says Jude uses the quote as a true cultural saying about God's judgment, without making 1 Enoch inspired. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=6093" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:41:33</a> | Is viewing explicit images evil? | He says it depends on purpose and context, but viewing for sexual gratification is sinful. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=6653" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:53</a> | Did Plato's Republic predict Jesus's crucifixion as Justin Martyr claims? | He remembers a passage that anticipates the idea but says it has been too long since he read the Republic to answer in detail. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=7267" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:01:07</a> | Is it plausible that Jesus read Greek philosophy? | He says Jesus could have read Greek and had access to libraries, but we do not know whether he read Greek philosophy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=8244" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:17:24</a> | Does the shift from Elohim to Yahweh Elohim mean Genesis 2:1-3 belongs with Genesis 1? | He says no, though there are other reasons some might connect the sections; ancient writers could shift deity names. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=9025" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:30:25</a> | Were Noah's sons retrojected into Genesis 5 before the flood, or named after later clan branches? | He says either is possible and the text does not give enough information to decide. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=9119" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:31:59</a> | What simple history book explains how average Jews lived? | He says he cannot recall a single good concentrated book because average Jewish life differs greatly by period. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=9259" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:34:19</a> | Is there evidence for the battle of the angel of the Lord in 2 Kings 19:35? | He takes the first stacked question and discusses Sennacherib's siege context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=9657" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:40:57</a> | Could gopher in Genesis 6:14 be an Akkadian cognate for reed or reed hut? | He says it is possible but uncertain, and that the main issue is not decisive for his reading. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=10712" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:58:32</a> | Is Hammurabi Nimrod? | He says no; Hammurabi is too late for Nimrod, whose identity remains uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=10864" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:01:04</a> | Did Mesopotamians also practice damnatio memoriae? | He says not as far as we can tell in the same metaphysical way Egyptians did. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=11504" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:11:44</a> | How do people get a destruction of Hazor from Judges 4-5 when the text never says the city was destroyed? | He says they want to connect Judges 4-5 with archaeological destruction layers, but the text itself does not say Hazor city was destroyed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=13297" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:41:37</a> | Where does he think Punt was located? | He places Punt broadly around Sudan, South Sudan, and northern Ethiopia, not Yemen. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=13683" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:48:03</a> | Is Hebrew from Eber? | He says no; that is a false-friend connection, and Hebrew is better connected with Habiru-like terminology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=13754" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:49:14</a> | Why was Tera forced to leave Kish? | He says the people of Kish wanted Elamite rather than Mari rule, and Tera was aligned with Mari. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=14001" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:53:21</a> | Are the sons of Eden related to the Garden of Eden? | He says he needed a reference, then checks 2 Kings 19:12 and treats the sons of Eden as a place-name issue rather than the garden. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=14157" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:55:57</a> | Why do modern believers not consider ancient origins and parallels of their religions? | He says the problem is often a false dichotomy that something must be either from God or pagan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=15068" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:11:08</a> | What is the ancient Near Eastern context behind Genesis 4:20-22? | He says the passage is ethnography, explaining origins of social groups and crafts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ZmyvBLejXL0?t=15818" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:23:38</a> | What was Samson's error besides marrying a Philistine woman? | He says Samson did not value the things of God and resembles the fool described in Proverbs. |
